statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a queue of integers. The first line contains the number of elements in the queue. The second line contains the elements themselves, space separated.
statement.outputFormat: Output is the dequeued integer, separated by a newline.
statement.notes: Note: Input is guaranteed to be valid.

hints.text: What happens when you dequeue the front element?

editorial.title: Solving the problem with a correct FIFO implementation.
editorial.intuition: Implementing a correct FIFO queue that dequeues the front element first is crucial to solving this problem. You can use a circular array or a linked list.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve checking every element in the queue to find the front element, which is inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves implementing a correct FIFO queue that keeps track of the front and rear elements correctly.
editorial.optimalSolution: An optimal solution involves implementing a circular array or a linked list to represent the queue, which allows for efficient addition and removal of elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: A proof of correctness involves showing that your implementation of the queue correctly follows the FIFO principle.
editorial.dryRun: Run a dry run with a small input to test your solution.
commonMistakes: Not implementing the FIFO principle correctly
